title: galium serpenticum subsp. warnerense
intro: Galium serpenticum subsp. warnerense, also known as Warner's bedstraw, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, due to its active compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In herbal preparations, Warner's bedstraw is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its potential medicinal and health benefits.
